description: Starter Phase 1 Agent Workspace guidance. Edit copy freely; re-run seed:guidance to upsert by slug/resourceType/nicheKey/clientAccountId.

resources.title: Final expense — opening script
resources.body:
Hi {{first_name}}, this is {{agent_name}} calling from {{agency_name}}. You had asked for information on final expense coverage that pays your family directly if something happens to you — does that ring a bell?

Great. I’m not here to sell you anything you don’t want; I just want to confirm a few details so I can see if what we have even makes sense. Do you have about two minutes right now, or should I call back at a better time?

resources.title: Veteran market — opening script
resources.body:
Hi {{first_name}}, {{agent_name}} here from {{agency_name}}. A lot of veterans we work with want a simple, no-nonsense review of benefit-style coverage options — not a pitch, just clarity. Is now still an okay time for a quick two-minute chat?

If yes: I’ll ask three quick questions so I don’t waste your time with the wrong information.

resources.title: Mortgage protection — opening script
resources.body:
Hi {{first_name}}, this is {{agent_name}} with {{agency_name}}. You were looking into options to help protect the mortgage if something unexpected happened — I’m calling to see if that’s still a priority for you.

If you have two minutes, I can outline how we usually structure this and you can tell me if it’s worth a closer look or not.

resources.title: IUL / cash-value — soft opener
resources.body:
Hi {{first_name}}, {{agent_name}} here. You asked about indexed-style life options that can build cash value over time. Before we go anywhere, I want to learn what you’re trying to solve — protection, tax-advantaged growth, supplemental retirement, or something else?

I’ll keep this conversational: a few questions, then I’ll tell you honestly if what we have fits.

resources.title: Health / Medicare-adjacent — careful opener
resources.body:
Hi {{first_name}}, this is {{agent_name}} from {{agency_name}}. I’m following up on your request for information. I’m not a government program, and I’m not calling about anything you didn’t ask for — I’m simply here to answer questions. Is now a good time for a quick two minutes?

(Adapt to your licensed scope and carrier-approved language.)

resources.title: Nurse / shift-worker — opening script
resources.body: Hi {{first_name}}, {{agent_name}} here. I know your schedule can be brutal, so I’ll be quick. You asked for info on coverage options — I’m calling to see if that’s still on your radar and what time window usually works best for a five-minute follow-up if now isn’t ideal.

resources.title: Trucker / mobile — opening script
resources.body: Hi {{first_name}}, this is {{agent_name}} with {{agency_name}}. I’ll keep this short since you might be on the road. You requested info on protecting your family if something happens to you — is this still a priority, and is there a safer time to talk for two minutes?

resources.title: General warm lead — opening script
resources.body:
Hi {{first_name}}, {{agent_name}} calling from {{agency_name}}. You had expressed interest in life insurance options — I’m following up to see if that’s still something you want to explore.

If you have two minutes, I’ll ask a few quick questions so I don’t recommend the wrong path. If now isn’t good, what day/time works better?

resources.title: Referral ask (natural close)
resources.body:
Before we wrap — a quick favor. Who do you know (family, coworker, neighbor) who should probably have this conversation but hasn’t gotten around to it?

I’m happy to treat them the same way I treated you: no pressure, just clarity. If you’re comfortable, you can text me their first name and the best window to reach them, or we can do a warm three-way intro.

resources.title: Beneficiary & emergency contact ask
resources.body:
Two quick housekeeping items so your file is complete:

1) Primary beneficiary — who should receive the benefit first, and what’s their relationship to you?
2) Contingent beneficiary — if something happened to both of you, who’s next in line?

Also: who should we list as an emergency contact (name + phone) if we can’t reach you during business hours?

resources.title: Annual policy review checklist
resources.body:
Annual review checklist (edit for your carriers/process):

- Confirm address, phone, email, and preferred contact method
- Verify beneficiaries still match intent (life events: marriage/divorce/birth)
- Review coverage amount vs current debts/income replacement goal
- Confirm premium mode (monthly/annual) and draft date
- Discuss any health or occupation changes since issue
- Confirm riders still needed (child rider, waiver, etc.)
- Set next review date and add to CRM

resources.title: Policy delivery checklist
resources.body:
Policy delivery checklist:

- Confirm identity and verify contact details
- Walk through declarations page: insured, face amount, term/permanent type, beneficiaries
- Explain premium amount, due dates, and grace period
- Set expectations for carrier welcome materials / portal registration
- Document client acknowledgement in CRM
- Schedule 30-day check-in for questions after they read the contract

resources.title: Underwriting — quick links (placeholder)
resources.body:
Replace this block with your org’s real links (carrier portals, e-app status, APS tracking, etc.).

Suggested placeholders:
- Carrier case status: <paste URL>
- Exam vendor / scheduling: <paste URL>
- Agent underwriting FAQ: <paste URL>

Do not store credentials here — use SSO or password manager links only.

resources.title: Follow-up after no answer
resources.body:
Voicemail script (30–45 seconds):

“Hi {{first_name}}, {{agent_name}} with {{agency_name}} — sorry I missed you. I’m following up on the life insurance info you requested. If that’s still a priority, call or text me at {{agent_phone}}. If your situation changed, just let me know and I’ll close your file. Thanks.”

Cadence suggestion: try a different time window before escalating frequency.

resources.title: Follow-up after appointment set
resources.body:
Text/email template after appointment is scheduled:

“{{first_name}}, confirming our appointment on {{appt_date}} at {{appt_time}} {{timezone}}. If anything changes on your end, reply to this message and I’ll adjust. I’ll send a quick reminder the day before.”

Add: what to bring (ID, beneficiary names/DOBs if known, medication list if medical underwriting applies).

resources.title: Follow-up after sale
resources.body:
Post-sale touch (24–48 hours):

“{{first_name}}, thanks again for trusting us. Quick recap: next steps are {{next_steps_in_plain_language}}. You’ll receive {{carrier}} materials by {{channel}}. If anything in the paperwork looks confusing, send me a photo and I’ll walk you through it.”

Schedule Day-30 wellness check and annual review reminder.

resources.title: No-show recovery script
resources.body:
Hi {{first_name}}, {{agent_name}} here — I had you on my calendar at {{appt_time}} and wasn’t able to connect. Everything okay?

No guilt, no pressure: if you still want help, we can reschedule for {{two_options}}. If priorities changed, I can close the loop — which is it?

resources.title: Think about it — follow-up script
resources.body:
Totally fair to want to think it through. When people say that, it usually means one of three things: price, need, or trust.

Which one is closest for you? (pause)

Here’s what I’ll do: I’ll answer that one concern directly. If it still doesn’t fit, we’ll shake hands digitally and I’ll stop following up — sound reasonable?

objectionPlaybooks.title: Price objection
objectionPlaybooks.recommendedResponse:
I hear you — nobody wants to pay a penny more than they have to. Most folks are surprised the monthly amount can be less than a streaming subscription once we match the benefit to the budget.

If it’s okay, I’ll ask one question: are you more worried about the payment today, or about whether your family would have enough if something happened tomorrow?
objectionPlaybooks.followUpMessage: Quick recap from our call: we looked at keeping the benefit meaningful while adjusting the amount/term to fit your budget. Text me if you want me to rerun numbers with a different monthly target.
objectionPlaybooks.nextBestAction: Re-quote with adjusted face amount or term; confirm budget ceiling.

objectionPlaybooks.title: Need to talk to spouse
objectionPlaybooks.recommendedResponse:
That makes sense — this is a two-person decision in most households. Would it help if I sent a one-page summary you can share, and we schedule a time when you’re both available?

I’m not trying to corner anyone; I just want both of you to hear the same facts.
objectionPlaybooks.followUpMessage: Following up: did you and your spouse get a chance to compare notes? I can do a 10-minute joint call or a three-way if that’s easier.
objectionPlaybooks.nextBestAction: Book joint appointment or send neutral summary PDF.

objectionPlaybooks.title: Think about it
objectionPlaybooks.recommendedResponse:
Of course — big decisions deserve thought. So I don’t guess wrong, what specifically do you want to think through: the need, the cost, or something about how the policy works?

If you tell me the one sticking point, I’ll address only that and you can decide next steps.
objectionPlaybooks.followUpMessage: Checking in on what you wanted to think through — happy to clarify one point without rehashing the whole call.
objectionPlaybooks.nextBestAction: Isolate objection; schedule concrete follow-up time.

objectionPlaybooks.title: Not interested
objectionPlaybooks.recommendedResponse:
Thanks for being direct — I appreciate it. Before I let you go: is it ‘not interested ever,’ or ‘not interested right now because timing/priority’?

If it’s never, I’ll close your file. If it’s timing, what would need to change for this to be worth revisiting?
objectionPlaybooks.nextBestAction: Respect opt-out; disposition accurately in CRM.

objectionPlaybooks.title: Already have coverage
objectionPlaybooks.recommendedResponse:
That’s good — coverage means someone cared enough to put protection in place. A lot of people still do a quick review to make sure the amount still matches the mortgage/kids’ ages/income today.

Would you be open to a five-minute ‘coverage check’: beneficiary correct, amount still enough, premium still competitive?
objectionPlaybooks.followUpMessage: If you find your old policy details, I can help you read the declarations page like a translator — no obligation.
objectionPlaybooks.nextBestAction: Offer review-only positioning; don’t invalidate existing coverage.

objectionPlaybooks.title: Call me back later
objectionPlaybooks.recommendedResponse:
Absolutely — what day and time usually works best for you? I’ll put it on my calendar and keep it short.

If I miss you, what’s the best number and window?
objectionPlaybooks.followUpMessage: You asked me to follow up — is now a better time for that two-minute conversation?
objectionPlaybooks.nextBestAction: Schedule explicit callback; send calendar confirmation.

objectionPlaybooks.title: Trust / skepticism
objectionPlaybooks.recommendedResponse:
I get it — there are a lot of salesy calls out there. Here’s how I like to work: I’ll explain options in plain English, I’ll tell you if something isn’t a fit, and I’ll never pressure you to decide on the spot.

If anything feels off, tell me and we’ll pause.
objectionPlaybooks.followUpMessage: If helpful, I can share our license numbers / carrier appointments / reviews so you know who you’re dealing with.
objectionPlaybooks.nextBestAction: Offer verification path; slow down and mirror concerns.

objectionPlaybooks.title: Too busy right now
objectionPlaybooks.recommendedResponse: Respect — I’ll be quick. If two minutes isn’t possible now, what’s a realistic window later today or tomorrow? I’ll call once, keep it tight, and if it’s not a fit we’ll part friends.
objectionPlaybooks.followUpMessage: Trying you at the time you suggested — if you need to reschedule, just text ‘later’.
objectionPlaybooks.nextBestAction: Anchor specific callback slot; keep promise on brevity.
